title: amaryllis × canaliculata
intro: Amaryllis × canaliculata, also known as the 'Red Lion' or 'King's Gold', is a hybrid plant species within the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its vibrant flowers and rich fragrance. The plant contains alkaloids such as galantamine, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Amaryllis × canaliculata has been used to promote digestion, treat respiratory issues, and even alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease due to its neuroprotective properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
